title: Overlap-aware validation with single-count tail scoring (maximal context)
author: nehowell
body:
This PR updates validation to score each token exactly once while allowing overlapping left-context windows.

### Summary

The current validation logic evaluates disjoint fixed-length windows. This limits the effective left context available to the model, particularly at window boundaries.

This change introduces overlapping evaluation windows stepped by EVAL_STRIDE, while ensuring that each target token is scored exactly once.

### Method

- Windows are constructed with up to TRAIN_SEQ_LEN tokens of left context
- Windows advance by EVAL_STRIDE (default = TRAIN_SEQ_LEN, preserving original behavior)
- Only the newly exposed tail region contributes to:
  - validation loss
  - byte accounting (BPB)
- Overlapping prefix tokens provide context only and are not double-counted

### Properties

- No change to training
- No change to dataset or ordering
- No future-token leakage
- Each validation token contributes exactly once
- Byte accounting semantics preserved

### Implementation notes

- Prefix loss is recomputed and subtracted for clarity and correctness
- This could be optimized further by masking per-token losses in a single forward pass

### Status

- Not benchmarked for SOTA submission
- Intended as a clean, correctness-focused evaluation improvement

### Motivation

Provides more realistic conditioning during evaluation by allowing each token to be predicted with maximal available left context, without inflating metrics.

---

Happy to iterate on implementation details if preferred.
